vision_report.biome_observations: Early frames show bare soil with some debris (white fragments, possibly perlite or eggshell). No active plant growth was observed in any frame prior to the blackout.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a progression from a stable, low-light observation state to a complete loss of visual data (blackout) starting at T-1 and persisting through the current state.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: Inconclusive. The specimens appear to have been in a state of 'bare soil' or 'dormancy' in the early frames, and the current total lack of visual data prevents any health assessment.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the total loss of visual input (blackout) in the final two frames. The early frames show no clear evidence of the registered plants, suggesting either extreme dormancy, recent planting, or misidentification of the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image stack. Initially, the desk environment contained four pots (P1-P4) with visible soil and a white rabbit anchor in P3. However, no distinct botanical specimens were identifiable in the early frames, suggesting the pots were either empty or contained only soil. From T-1 to the current state, the camera feed has failed, resulting in a total blackout. Consequently, I cannot confirm the current status of the biome or the health of the plants.
